structure.notes: The note preserves the partial-identification locked tuple, includes the required Stage 0 sections, labels assumptions, separates the target ATE from the bounding functional, and gives an actionable math-object checklist.

novelty.notes: The current derivation is a useful sharp finite-envelope reduction, but it does not meet novelty_target=flagship: the admitted unproved five-family facet taxonomy and strict-shrinkage frontier are precisely the more ambitious flagship-level claims, and the note does not show that the one-dimensional cap-envelope opens a previously unresolved regime relative to Balke--Pearl, Richardson--Robins, or automated discrete-bound methods.

correctness.notes: The principal-strata parametrization, fixed-t interval reduction, endpoint algebra, compactness/sharpness argument, face-limit proposition, and numerical minimality example are mathematically coherent under the stated assumptions.

perItemFindings.label: Main theorem / novelty tier
perItemFindings.one_line: Assessed tier is subfield below novelty_target=flagship; the theorem proves an explicit reduction of an established finite LP with two mass caps, but not a regime-opening flagship contribution.

perItemFindings.label: Conjecture 1
perItemFindings.one_line: The note explicitly only partially confirms the proposed nonredundant five-family facet taxonomy and strict-shrinkage catalogue, so it has not proved every flagship conjectural component.

perItemFindings.label: Related work and positioning
perItemFindings.one_line: The comparison explains that the result differs from Balke--Pearl and fixed-defier sensitivity work, but it does not identify a published open problem or prove a strict extension to a wider class as required for flagship tier.

verbatim_critique: Structure passes: the artifact has the required sections, the locked parameter block correctly identifies a PartialID anchor with observed distribution P_{Y,D,Z}, target theta_ATE, IV+BDN restrictions, and a bounding functional, and Section 14 separates probabilistic objects from deterministic algebraic objects. Correctness also passes on the current theorem: solving the first-stage equations by t=pi_F gives pi_A=q_{01}-t, pi_N=q_{10}-t, pi_C=q_{00}-q_{10}+t; the x and w interval constraints in Section 7 exactly encode support for the observed outcome equations; substituting into theta_ATE gives R-x+w+y_{1N}-y_{0A}; and the lower and upper fixed-t choices produce B_L(t)=R-U_x(t)+L_w(t)-a(t) and B_U(t)=R-L_x(t)+U_w(t)+n(t). The compact-polytope and piecewise-affine knot arguments in Section 11 are sufficient for sharpness and computability, and the Section 13 numerical example correctly shows nonredundancy of the never-taker cap. The revision is therefore not a mathematical repair of the proved envelope, but a novelty-floor repair. The orchestrator requires flagship. The current theorem is an explicit one-dimensional evaluation of a binary-IV partial-identification LP with two additional mass caps. That is useful, but the note itself says Conjecture 1 is only partially confirmed as to the nonredundant five-family taxonomy and strict-shrinkage catalogue, and it replaces the proposed closed-form facet catalogue by an active-knot enumeration. It also does not show that Balke--Pearl, Richardson--Robins, symbolic-bound methods, or monotonicity-violation sensitivity papers left this simultaneous defier/never-taker-cap regime open in the flagship sense. To meet the floor, Stage 0 would need to prove the stronger finite affine facet catalogue and the necessary-and-sufficient strict-shrinkage frontier, or otherwise position and prove the result as a genuine regime-opening theorem against a named published gap. Without that upgrade, the current derivation should be treated as subfield-tier rather than accepted under a flagship target.
